titleStmt.title: Keiyo Tei Bunko hi (mintaku) — Zheng Wengong Stele, Ming rubbing (rubbing)
けい陽鄭文公碑（明拓）（拓本）

msIdentifier.repository: Japan Search (ジャパンサーチ) — national aggregator

msContents.summary: Ink rubbing of a Zheng Daozhao 鄭道昭 (Tei Dōshō) Daoist cliff inscription: Keiyo Tei Bunko hi (mintaku) — Zheng Wengong Stele, Ming rubbing. Holding institution: Kyoto National Museum 京都国立博物館 (ColBase / NICH), via Japan Search. 鄭文公碑 (Tei Bunko hi) is Zheng Daozhao's most famous stele. Record page verified HTTP 200. CAUTION: the thumbnail URL supplied by the Japan Search/ColBase API (media/.../slideshow_s/31253_difile1-L.jpg) returned 404, and ColBase exposes no working IIIF manifest (the /manifest endpoint serves SPA HTML); therefore classified record-only rather than image. Licence stated as CC-BY in the index.

support.objectType: 拓片 · ink rubbing on paper

origin.origDate: Inscription 5th-6th c. (carved 511); this is a Ming-dynasty 明拓 rubbing; 1 folding album, 32.5x20.1cm

bibl.ref: Japan Search (ジャパンサーチ) — national aggregator — record

revisionDesc.change: Zheng Daozhao 鄭道昭 (Tei Dōshō) Daoist cliff inscription; harvested from Japan Search (ジャパンサーチ) — national aggregator via the Epiwen Japanese-repository workflow. Provenance-only (no public IIIF image).

div.p: Ink rubbing — see the linked record/manifest.
